description: A world where ancient Murim cultivation meets arcane magic — the Merged Realms, born from a cataclysm that shattered two worlds together.

locations.name: Mount Tianlun — The Jade Palace
locations.description: The sacred peak of the Murim Alliance, a fortress-monastery carved into the mountain face. Training grounds, council chambers, and the Azure Cloud Sect's former home.

locations.name: The Spire of Convergence
locations.description: A hybrid tower of impossible architecture — half-Murim pagoda, half-arcane spire — floating above Mount Tianlun. The Arcane Council headquarters.

locations.name: The Convergence Market
locations.description: Sprawling city-sized bazaar at the base of Mount Tianlun. Anything can be bought or sold here.
locations.short_description: The economic heart of the Merged Realms

locations.name: The Black Mountains — Abyss Palace
locations.description: The corrupted fortress of the Heavenly Demon Cult, built into the caldera of an active volcano.
locations.short_description: Headquarters of the Heavenly Demon Cult

locations.name: The Southern Kingdoms
locations.description: Three mortal kingdoms — Yan, Wei, and Zhao — united under the Tri-Kingdom Compact.

locations.name: The Serpent River
locations.description: The heavily fortified front line between Alliance and Cult territories.

locations.name: The Verdant Wound
locations.description: The largest open rift — a miles-long tear in reality hanging in the sky.

locations.name: The Silverwood Forest
locations.description: A vast forest of silver-barked trees glowing with bioluminescent magic, slowly dying from the Withering.

locations.name: The Floating Isles of Aerendor
locations.description: Seven sky-islands drifting above the Crimson Desert, home to mages and scholars.

locations.name: The Crimson Desert
locations.description: Red sand desert with Dominion ruins being uncovered by shifting sands.

locations.name: The Whispering Wastes
locations.description: A desolate wasteland where sands whisper secrets. Home to necromancers and exiles.

locations.name: The Dragon's Teeth Mountains
locations.description: A volcanic mountain range ruled by the Ebon Scale Covenant.

locations.name: The Bridge of Sighs
locations.description: A massive thirty-mile stone bridge connecting to the Isle of Whispers.

locations.name: The Isle of Whispers
locations.description: The largest surviving fragment of the Arcane Dominion with First Age ruins.

locations.name: The Heartwood Nexus
locations.description: The only known location where qi and mana have achieved permanent stable harmony.

locations.name: Necropolis Omega
locations.description: The hidden capital of the Pale Hand — a city of the dead beneath the Whispering Wastes.

npcs.name: Song Linfeng
npcs.title: Grand Elder of the Murim Alliance
npcs.description: A weathered but dignified man with silver hair. The pragmatic leader who holds the Alliance together.
npcs.appearance: White robes with gold embroidery, tired eyes.
npcs.greeting: Welcome to the Jade Palace. I pray you bring good news.

dialogue_topics.alliance: The Alliance is all that stands between order and chaos.
dialogue_topics.war: The Cult presses us from the west.

npcs.name: Xuan Mo
npcs.title: The Crimson Demon Lord
npcs.description: Tall, gaunt, with pale skin and crimson eyes. Ruthless leader of the Cult.
npcs.appearance: Black-and-crimson robes embroidered with writhing souls.
npcs.greeting: You have come to die — or to serve.

dialogue_topics.ascension: I will consume the Convergence Zones and become a god.

npcs.name: Seraphina Vex
npcs.title: Grand Archmage of the Arcane Council
npcs.description: A tall, slender woman with silver-white hair and piercing blue eyes.
npcs.appearance: Deep blue and silver robes, crystalline staff.
npcs.greeting: Welcome to the Spire. I trust your business is meaningful.

dialogue_topics.magic: Magic must be studied, understood, and regulated.

npcs.name: Queen Alyndra Moonshadow
npcs.title: Sovereign of the Silverwood
npcs.description: An ethereally beautiful high elf with silver hair and luminous green eyes.
npcs.appearance: Crown of living branches, robes of woven moonlight.

dialogue_topics.elves: My people are all that remain of a lost world.

npcs.name: Deng Jie
npcs.title: Founder of the Rift Wardens
npcs.description: A stocky, scarred man with a shaved head and friendly face.
npcs.appearance: Leather armor, steel sword, tired eyes.
npcs.greeting: Need a job? The Wardens always have contracts.

dialogue_topics.wardens: We clear rifts, hunt monsters, and get paid.
dialogue_topics.danger: The Verdant Wound gets worse every day.

npcs.name: Valthorax
npcs.title: The Ember King
npcs.description: A massive red dragon, scales gleaming like polished rubies.
npcs.appearance: In human form: a tall man with ruby-red eyes.
npcs.greeting: A mortal who dares approach my lair.

dialogue_topics.merge: The Merge is the most interesting thing in millennia.
dialogue_topics.power: Do not mistake my patience for weakness.

npcs.name: Lich Lord Mortis
npcs.title: Leader of the Pale Hand
npcs.description: A skeletal figure with cold blue pinpricks of light for eyes.
npcs.appearance: Bones wrapped in shadow, dragon-spine staff.

npcs.name: Kaede the Windblade
npcs.title: Leader of the Wandering Sword Association
npcs.description: A tall, lean woman with weather-beaten skin and sharp eyes.
npcs.appearance: Traveler's clothes, a long curved sword.

dialogue_topics.swords: The blade is an extension of the self.

npcs.name: Yun Mei
npcs.title: The First Harmonized
npcs.description: A young woman with unruly black hair and ink-stained hands.
npcs.appearance: Traveler's clothes, backpack full of books.
npcs.greeting: Oh! A visitor! I love answering questions.

dialogue_topics.harmonization: Qi and mana can flow together.
dialogue_topics.research: I've discovered the key is in the soul.

npcs.name: Grand Elder Feng Wei
npcs.title: Voice of the Lost Age
npcs.description: A legendary Nascent Soul cultivator who perished in the Merge. His spirit lingers.
npcs.appearance: A translucent figure in flowing robes.
npcs.greeting: I am but a memory now.

dialogue_topics.merging: Before the Rending, we thought we understood the sky.
dialogue_topics.sacrifice: I pray my sacrifice was not in vain.

factions.name: Murim Alliance
factions.description: The governing body of orthodox martial society — a coalition of sects, clans, and kingdoms.

factions.name: Heavenly Demon Cult
factions.description: A shadow empire of demonic cultivators who consume life essence.

factions.name: Arcane Council
factions.description: The governing body of magic — nine circles of mastery regulating spellcraft.

factions.name: Silverwood Dominion
factions.description: Elven refugees of a dying forest, struggling against the Withering.

factions.name: Ebon Scale Covenant
factions.description: The ruling council of ancient dragons in the Dragon's Teeth Mountains.

factions.name: Rift Wardens
factions.description: A neutral guild of hunters who clear rifts for coin.

factions.name: Unorthodox
factions.description: Independent sects, wandering martial artists, and mercenaries who value freedom.

factions.name: Pale Hand
factions.description: Necromancers who practice forbidden arts of death, allied with the Cult.

quests.name: Defend the Spire
quests.description: The Cult is assaulting the Spire of Convergence. Join the defense!

quests.name: Find Elder Feng Liang
quests.description: The missing Alliance elder entered ancient ruins. Find him.

objectives.description: Travel to the Dragon's Teeth Mountains

quests.name: Hunt the Alpha Magi-Beast
quests.description: An intelligent magi-beast terrorizes the Verdant Wound region.

quests.name: The Heartwood Sabotage
quests.description: Cult infiltrators threaten to corrupt the Heartwood Nexus.

quests.name: The Third Convergence Zone
quests.description: Find the hidden third Convergence Zone beneath the mountains.

quests.name: Whispering Wastes Expedition
quests.description: Map Necropolis Omega and gather intelligence on the Pale Hand.

bosses.name: Xuan Mo's Shadow Clone
bosses.title: Manifestation of the Crimson Demon Lord
bosses.description: Not the Demon Lord himself — his demonic inner world made flesh. A perfect replica of Xuan Mo forged from condensed demonic qi, capable of using every technique he has mastered. Destroying it will not kill Xuan Mo, but it will wound his soul and weaken the Cult's hold over the western territories.

2.name: Crimson Demonic Armament
2.description: The clone draws on deeper reserves — a corona of crimson demonic qi erupts around it. All attacks now deal an additional 2d10 necrotic damage. The clone becomes immune to being frightened or charmed. Any creature that hits it in melee takes 1d8 fire damage from the feedback.

3.name: Heavenly Demon Descent
3.description: The clone abandons all restraint — it channels the true Heavenly Demon Manifestation. Its size increases, its attacks become AoE (all creatures in 10 ft take damage), and it gains 5 legendary actions per round. The battlefield is shrouded in crimson mist — all non-cultist creatures have disadvantage on saving throws.

legendary_actions.name: Soul Slash
legendary_actions.description: A crescent of demonic qi strikes one target for 2d10+7 slashing + 1d8 necrotic damage.

legendary_actions.name: Thousand Soul Convergence
legendary_actions.description: Xuan Mo's clone projects its demonic intent — all creatures within 20 ft make DC 18 WIS save or be frightened until end of their next turn.

legendary_actions.name: Demonic Blood Art
legendary_actions.description: The clone sacrifices 20 HP to detonate a sphere of blood qi — 30-ft radius, 5d10 necrotic damage, DC 17 CON save for half. Creatures that fail are stunned until end of their next turn.

lair_actions.name: Demonic Qi Surge
lair_actions.description: Waves of corrupted energy wash through the arena — all non-Cult creatures make DC 16 CON save or have their speed halved and take 2d8 necrotic damage.

lair_actions.name: Soul Anchor
lair_actions.description: The clone reaches into one creature's soul — that creature cannot move more than 30 ft from their current position until initiative count 20 on the next round. DC 17 WIS save to negate.

lair_actions.name: Cult Reinforcements
lair_actions.description: 1d4 Cult disciples (AC 13, 22 HP, +4 attack, 1d8+2 damage) are drawn through a rift in the demonic qi and enter combat.

bosses.name: Lich Lord Mortis — Risen
bosses.title: The Pale Hand Ascendant
bosses.description: The true form of the Lich Lord Mortis when he stops holding back. The leader of the Pale Hand drops all pretense of restraint and channels every ounce of necrotic power accumulated over centuries of forbidden practice. His phylactery is hidden inside Necropolis Omega — defeat him in combat and he will reform, but each defeat chips away at his essence.

2.name: Bone Choir Awakens
2.description: Mortis calls upon the collective consciousness of the Pale Hand — the voices of a thousand dead liches speak through him. He gains immunity to necrotic damage. His attacks now drain 1d6 from the target's maximum HP (recovers on long rest). The chamber fills with spectral wailing — all creatures must make a DC 16 WIS save at the start of each turn or lose their bonus action.

3.name: The Death Transcendent
3.description: Mortis fully channels the Outer Gate's power — he becomes partially extradimensional. He gains resistance to all damage except radiant, force, and magical weapon damage. All of his spells are cast as if Empowered and Quickened. The air in the room becomes deadly still — any creature that starts their turn with less than half HP must make a DC 18 CON save or drop to 0 HP.

legendary_actions.name: Necromantic Bolt
legendary_actions.description: One creature takes 2d10+5 necrotic damage. If this kills the target, they rise as a zombie (AC 8, 22 HP) under Mortis's control.

legendary_actions.name: Life Drain
legendary_actions.description: One creature within 60 ft must succeed on DC 17 CON save or take 4d8+5 necrotic damage and have their HP maximum reduced by the same amount until a long rest. Mortis regains HP equal to half the damage dealt.

legendary_actions.name: Army of Bone
legendary_actions.description: Mortis animates the corpses of any creatures that have died in the encounter — each rises as a skeleton (AC 13, 13 HP, +4 attack, 1d6+2) under his control.

lair_actions.name: Death Pulse
lair_actions.description: A wave of negative energy ripples through the Necropolis — all living creatures take 3d8 necrotic damage, DC 15 CON save for half. Undead in the area are healed for 15 HP instead.

lair_actions.name: Spectral Wall
lair_actions.description: A barrier of screaming spirits rises across any wall, door, or passageway of Mortis's choice — it blocks movement and deals 2d10 necrotic to any creature that passes through it. Lasts until initiative count 20 of the next round.

lair_actions.name: Temporal Stasis
lair_actions.description: One creature is frozen in time — they skip their next turn entirely. DC 19 INT save to resist. This cannot affect the same creature twice in a row.

bosses.name: Valthorax — The Ember Sovereign
bosses.title: The Ember King Unchained
bosses.description: When diplomacy ends and Valthorax decides a threat is worthy of his full power, the Ember King's human form burns away and the true dragon emerges. A creature of apocalyptic scale whose wings blot out the sun above the Dragon's Teeth Mountains. Valthorax rarely enters this form — the last time he did, he leveled a city. The fact that he's doing it again means someone has made a very serious mistake.

2.name: Sovereign's Wrath
2.description: Valthorax reaches his full power — the mountain itself trembles. His fire breath deals maximum damage to the first target it hits. He gains the ability to use Fire Breath and a legendary action in the same turn. Any creature that deals more than 30 damage to him in a single hit must make a DC 20 STR save or be hurled 30 ft into a wall (taking 3d6 bludgeoning damage). The sky above the battlefield turns red.

legendary_actions.name: Detect
legendary_actions.description: Valthorax makes a Perception check — all hidden, invisible, or magically concealed creatures within 120 ft are revealed to him until his next turn.

legendary_actions.name: Tail Strike
legendary_actions.description: One creature within 20 ft takes 2d8+10 bludgeoning damage, DC 23 STR save or be knocked prone and pushed 15 ft.

legendary_actions.name: Wing Tempest
legendary_actions.description: Valthorax beats his wings — all creatures within 20 ft take 2d8+10 bludgeoning damage and must make a DC 21 STR save or be knocked back 20 ft and knocked prone. Valthorax may then fly up to half his speed.

legendary_actions.name: Ember Sovereign's Breath
legendary_actions.description: 90-ft cone of superheated dragon fire — 20d6 fire damage, DC 24 DEX save for half. The ground in the area becomes scorched earth (difficult terrain, 3d6 fire damage to anyone who enters). Recharge 5–6.

lair_actions.name: Volcanic Eruption
lair_actions.description: The mountain responds to Valthorax's rage — lava geysers erupt from six points of the GM's choice within the lair. Each creates a 10-ft radius zone of molten rock (4d10 fire damage on contact, 5 ft costs 15 ft of movement). Lasts 2 rounds.

lair_actions.name: Magma Hail
lair_actions.description: Chunks of molten rock rain from the ceiling — all creatures make DC 19 DEX save or take 4d6 bludgeoning + 2d6 fire damage and are covered in burning magma (1d6 fire at start of each turn until they use an action to scrape it off).

lair_actions.name: Primordial Roar
lair_actions.description: Valthorax channels the ancient primordial dragon sleeping beneath the mountain — his roar shakes reality. All creatures make DC 22 CON save or be stunned until initiative count 10 of the next round. Creatures within 10 ft automatically fail.

lore_entries.title: The Day the Sky Broke
lore_entries.content: On the 15th day of the 7th Moon, the sky turned violet and shattered — the Merging of two worlds.

lore_entries.title: Qi — The Internal Energy
lore_entries.content: Qi is the lifeblood of the Murim world. Cultivators refine this internal energy across stages from Body Tempering to Dao Integration.

lore_entries.title: Mana — The Arcane Force
lore_entries.content: Mana is the raw fabric of magic. Unlike qi which flows within, mana permeates the environment.

lore_entries.title: The First Harmonized
lore_entries.content: Yun Mei discovered she could channel qi through a spell matrix, creating the first Harmonized practitioner.

lore_entries.title: The Great Convergence Zones
lore_entries.content: Three locations where qi and mana have achieved harmony: the Spire, the Heartwood Nexus, and a hidden third.

lore_entries.title: The Heavenly Demon Cult
lore_entries.content: A 3,000-year-old shadow empire. Under Xuan Mo they corrupt mana into demonic techniques.

lore_entries.title: The Withering of the Silverwood
lore_entries.content: The qi-rich atmosphere of the Murim world is slowly killing the Silverwood Forest — a blight called the Withering.

lore_entries.title: The Voice in the Bone Choir
lore_entries.content: The true ruler of the Pale Hand is a collective consciousness of all liches.

lore_entries.title: The Outer Gate
lore_entries.content: Ten thousand years ago, cultivators attempted to open a gateway beyond reality. Something came through.

lore_entries.title: The Sleeper Beneath
lore_entries.content: A fragment of the Primordial Dragon slumbers beneath the Dragon's Teeth Mountains.

lore_entries.title: The Architects of the Merge
lore_entries.content: Seraphina Vex discovered the Merge was a weapon deployed by a civilization that predates both worlds.

lore_entries.title: Rift Dungeons
lore_entries.content: Stabilized rifts became permanent gateways to pocket dimensions filled with treasures and monsters.
